html:
Abstract

This document serves as a comprehensive user guide for Karl – Insight Agent (Kanerika Inc.), outlining the complete lifecycle from trial access to full product adoption and operational usage. It details the trial activation process, feature scope during the evaluation period, usage limitations, and administrative controls. The guide further explains subscription models, purchasing workflow, licensing configuration, and payment enablement. Additionally, it provides step-by-step instructions for onboarding, workspace setup, user management, and day-to-day platform utilization to ensure seamless adoption and effective enterprise deployment.

Karl – Insight Agent
User Manual

1. Overview

In today’s modern data landscape, enterprises operate across heterogeneous data estates — spanning cloud data platforms, on-premises systems, SaaS applications, and governed analytics layers. While cloud ecosystems such as Microsoft Azure provide robust foundational services for ingestion, transformation, and analytics, organizations increasingly require an intelligence layer that abstracts complexity and enables business users to interact with data intuitively, securely, and at scale.

Karl – Insight Agent, developed by Kanerika Inc., is designed to serve as that intelligence layer.

1.1 Karl – Insight Agent

Karl – Insight Agent is an enterprise-grade AI analytics platform that enables natural language–driven data analysis, visualization, and insights generation over governed enterprise data. The system is designed with a modular, secure, and scalable architecture, leveraging Microsoft Fabric as the central data and analytics backbone. It integrates:

  • Natural Language–driven analytics orchestration using large language models for intent understanding and reasoning.
  • Semantic retrieval and context-aware processing to enrich user queries with relevant data and historical context.
  • Dynamic workflow orchestration, where the backend determines the appropriate execution path based on user intent.

2. Requirements and Environment Readiness

Before you begin, ensure you have the following:

    1. Access to a Microsoft Fabric workspace with Member permissions or higher.
    2. A sample workload instance.

1. Create a Sample Workload

To enable Karl in your tenant please follow the enablement document provided here.

  1. Navigate to your desired Microsoft Fabric workspace.
  2. Click on New item
  3. From the list, search for and select sample item.
  4. Click on it.

2. Configure Authentication

To grant this workload access to your Azure resources, you must provide Azure Active Directory credentials.

  1. In the Workload, enter the following required credentials:
    1. Tenant ID: The ID of your Azure AD tenant.
    2. Client ID: The Application (Client) ID of your Service Principal.
    3. Client Secret: The secret value for the Service Principal.
  2. Click Connect to authenticate.

3. Trial Experience Guide

3.1 Purpose of Trial

The purpose of providing a free trial enables users to evaluate the platform’s capabilities, usability, and business relevance before making a financial commitment. It reduces adoption risk, builds trust, and accelerates decision-making by allowing stakeholders to validate real-world value using their own data or controlled datasets.

3.2 Trial Entry and Activation

Step 1: Enable Additional Workloads (One-Time Setting)

Before you can add the migration tool, ensure that custom workloads are enabled in your Fabric tenant:

  1. In Fabric, navigate to Settings > Admin Portal.
  2. Ensure the setting is enabled (or contact your tenant admin to enable it).

Step 2: Add the Workload from Workload Hub

  1. Go to the Workload Hub (you can find it in the Fabric navigation menu).
  1. Search for “Karl”
  1. Click Learn More to install the workload. You can add it either to a specific capacity or directly to a workspace where you intend to use it.

4. Click on Add.

Step 3: Start the Trial Through UI

  1. Inside KARL’s workload UI, users will see a “Try Now” or “Start Trial” button in the toolbar.
    • This is implemented using a Ribbon action that follows the Microsoft Fabric trial pattern.

Step 4: Trial Activation Dialog

When the user clicks “Start Trial”, KARL launches a trial dialog that:

  • Explains the trial duration (30 days)
  • Lists included credits and usage guidance
  • Shows any prerequisite steps or feature limits

This dialog is implemented using the Fabric trial template pattern (fabric-trial-experience) so it’s consistent with other Fabric workloads.

Step 5: What Happens After Trial Starts

After the user confirms in the trial dialog:

  1. The trial is activated in the backend.
  2. The user sees KARL’s main interface.
  1. The UI displays a trial banner indicating the remaining trial expiration date.

4. Click on “select your Lakehouse” and you will get the list of Lakehouses available.

5. Select the desired Lakehouse and Click on “Choose”

Step 6: How Users Use KARL During Trial

Once the KARL trial is active, users can:

  • Ask questions in English — KARL will automatically convert them to NL2SQL queries
  • Generate charts and visuals automatically
  • Do data Q&A discovery
  • Run “Why” analysis for deeper insight

Each action consumes trial credits. A credits status indicator is visible within KARL UI so users understand how much trial capacity remains.

3.3 Trial Capabilities and Limits

During the 30‑day trial, you have full access to the tool’s features, subject to the following limits:

Capability

Trial Limit

Duration

30 days from the moment you start the trial

Credits Limit

5100 Credits (included in free trial)

These limits allow you to validate the tool against real workloads while ensuring fair usage during evaluation. If you attempt to put queries after exhaustion of 5100 credits, you will be prompted to upgrade.

3.4 Trial Duration and Notifications

The tool keeps you informed of your trial status through clear visual indicators:

  • Home screen banner – Shows the number of days remaining in your trial (e.g., “14 days left for trial”).
  • Authentication screen banner – When fewer than five days remain, a reminder keeps appearing on the main configuration page, along with an Upgrade Now option.

These notifications ensure you are never surprised by the trial ending and can plan your upgrade accordingly.

3.5 Upgrading from the Trial

When your trial credits exhaust or you need to continue after the 30‑day period, upgrading is straightforward.

  • Conversion path: Click Upgrade from any of the Karl Home page. You will be taken to the Azure Marketplace listing for the Karl – Insight Agent, where you can select a paid plan.
  • Payment options: You can pay by credit card or link the subscription to your Azure subscription (invoiced through Azure).
  • Seamless transition: After completing the purchase, return to your Fabric workspace. The tool automatically detects the active subscription, removes the trial limits, and displays the licensed version.

3.6 What Happens After the Trial Ends?

If your trial expires (either by reaching 30 days or by hitting credit limit) without an upgrade:

  • You will no longer be able to execute new migrations.
  • Existing insights will still remain available, but new query generation is disabled.
  • A dialog prompts you to upgrade, clearly explaining the next steps.

You can always choose to remove the trial artifacts or contact our support team for assistance.

We hope this trial gives you the confidence to adopt the Karl – Insight Agent for your production workloads. If you have any questions during your evaluation, please reach out to our support portal at Support Portal .


4. User Navigation in Azure Marketplace

1. Open Microsoft Fabric

  • Log in to your Microsoft Fabric environment.
  • Navigate to the Workloads section from the left sidebar.
  • Locate Karl - Insights (preview) in the list of available workloads.

2. Go to Azure Marketplace

  • On the Karl workload page, click on “Azure Marketplace Offer Page” button, it will redirect you to the Azure Marketplace.
  • Scroll to the bottom of the Marketplace listing and click "Get it now” on Azure Portal.

3. Subscribe via Azure Portal

  • You’ll be redirected to the Azure Portal subscription page.
  • Select your Azure subscription and create or choose a resource group.
  • Choose the Free Tier plan:
  • 30-Day Free Trial
  • 5,100 Credits included

4. Disable Auto-Renewal

  • To ensure the subscription ends after the free trial, toggle Auto-renew to Off.

5. Review and Subscribe

  • Click Review + Subscribe.
  • On the next page, click "Configure account now".
  • You’ll also receive this link via email.
  • Clicking this triggers the subscription activation (may time to take a few minutes).

6. Return to Fabric

  • After activation, you’ll be redirected back to the Karl workload page.
  • You can now go to any of your workspaces and add Karl as a new item.
  • The workload will be available under your tenant's ID.

Credit System (Free Tier)

You receive 5,100 credits during the trial. Here's how credits are consumed:

Action Type

Credits Used

Extraction

1

Start Visualization

After Extraction

QnA

3

Chart Gen/Edit

7

Why Analysis

45

Deep Analysis

60

This guide ensures users can smoothly activate and begin using Karl for AI-powered data insights in Microsoft Fabric.

5. Workload Manual

Step 1: After signing in, the user can select the desired Microsoft Fabric workspace. In this case, our workspace is “Karl – Insight Agent”.

Step 2: Click on “New Item”

  • Search for “Karl – Insight Agent (preview)”
  • Click on it.
  • If the Tenant ID is not found in the database, the user will receive an error indicating the “Tenant ID is not present.”
  • If the Tenant ID is found but the associated subscription has expired, the user will receive an error message stating “Subscription/Trial has expired.”.

Step 3: Create Karl Item

Step 4: The Workload Interface will load.

  1. Click on “Select Your Lakehouse”
  2. This will display all the available Lakehouses in the particular Workspace
  3. Select the desired Lakehouse and Click on “Choose”

Step 5: Once the KARL trial is active, users can:

  • Ask questions in English — KARL will automatically convert them to NL2SQL queries
  • Generate charts and visuals automatically
  • Do data Q&A discovery
  • Run “Why” analysis for deeper insight

6. Support and Service Level Agreement

6.1 Support Contact

For technical assistance, migration support, or to report issues, contact:
Kanerika Support Portal: https://kanerika.com/datasheet-page/kar-data-intelligence-agent/

E-Mail: karl.insight.support@kanerika.com

6.2 Service Level Agreement

6.2.1 Priority Level Definitions

Kanerika utilizes a tiered incident response logic to address technical urgency and business impact within the Microsoft Fabric environment.

  • P0 – Critical: Total System Outage. A complete failure that prevents all users from accessing the workload or blocks all analysis capabilities.
    • Example 1: A total failure of the Fabric Workload SDK integration, preventing the Karl interface from loading within the Fabric portal.
    • Example 2: A complete failure of the Usage & Subscription DB (PostgreSQL RDS), which prevents the credit system from authorizing any user operations.
  • P1 – High: Major Functionality Degradation. Critical features are unavailable to multiple users with no viable workaround.
    • Example 1: Failure of the Deep Analysis or Why Analysis modules, halting high-resource, credit-intensive diagnostic operations.
    • Example 2: Synchronization failures between the Azure Marketplace subscription and the Fabric environment, causing incorrect credit exhaustion errors.
  • P2 – Medium: Partial Disruption. Functional errors exist, but a viable workaround allows the user to continue activities.
    • Example 1: Errors in specific Plotly chart generations where the user can still access the raw data via the "Technical View."
    • Example 2: Intermittent latency in SQL generation that can be resolved by re-submitting the natural language query.
  • P3 – Low: General Inquiries. Minor usability issues, documentation clarifications, or enhancement requests.
    • Example 1: Clarification requests regarding training data CRUD operations.
    • Example 2: Suggestions for UI/UX cosmetic improvements.

6.2.2 Response Time Targets and Performance Commitments

In cloud-native environments, time-bound acknowledgments are necessary to maintain enterprise trust. Kanerika commits to the following initial response time targets:

Priority Level

Initial Response Time

P0 – Critical

Within 4 hours

P1 – High

Within 8 hours

P2 – Medium

Within 24 hours

P3 – Low

Within 4 business days

Definition of Response Time: The duration required for Kanerika support personnel to acknowledge receipt of the ticket, assess the technical severity, and initiate a formal investigation.

External Dependencies: While Kanerika strives for rapid resolution, resolution times are subject to external variables. These include Azure OpenAI GPT-4 rate limits, OneLake availability, and Microsoft Fabric capacity constraints
